summary: Iter 11 — P1 Alerts (Telegram + SMTP email for HIGH-severity audit events) is GREEN end-to-end. Backend: 12/12 new pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_alerts_iter11.py (defaults, PUT round-trip, test endpoint 400 with TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/SMTP_* error messages, invalid channel, auth gating across 4 endpoints, KILL_SWITCH auto-dispatch produces alert_log rows for telegram+email with ok=false and proper error reasons, 60s dedup window collapses 3 rapid ARM toggles to 1 row per channel, admin/health.alerts block exposes telegram/email/global_telegram/global_email). Backend regression: 17/17 iter10 admin tests still pass. Frontend: full Playwright E2E — login → /settings → both tabs (RISK & PROFILE, ALERTS) render → alerts panel shows NO BOT TOKEN + NO SMTP red badges → both inputs editable → 5/5 default event chips active and toggleable → SEND TEST buttons correctly disabled for both unconfigured transports → SAVE ALERT PREFERENCES emits 'Alert preferences saved' toast → reload confirms telegram_chat_id (555111222) persisted → regression nav to /admin /backtest /paper /audit /trap /brokers all render. Live alert_log on settings page also visible with FAIL KILL_SWITCH rows confirming auto-dispatch from kill-switch toggles wired correctly.

issues: Row layout uses flex justify-between with 5 children; on narrow viewports columns squeeze and destination column truncates aggressively. Acceptable but a CSS grid with fixed column widths would render cleaner.
No empty-state hint when transports are unconfigured beyond the red status pill — consider an inline tooltip on SEND TEST explaining why it is disabled (currently button is just greyed).

action_items: (Optional polish) Add an inline tooltip/help text on disabled SEND TEST buttons explaining missing transport env vars.

(Optional UX) AlertsPanel save() always overwrites event_types/min_severity even when user only changed one field — currently fine because state stays in sync, but consider PATCH semantics if pref schema grows.
(Optional infra) services/alerts.py uses an in-process OrderedDict for dedup — won't dedup across multiple uvicorn workers in production. Move to a Mongo TTL collection or Redis if scaling out.

(Optional infra) Telegram/SMTP env vars TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD/FROM, TELEGRAM_GLOBAL_CHAT_ID, SMTP_GLOBAL_RECIPIENT remain intentionally unset — when these are wired in production, also add unit-tests that monkeypatch httpx/aiosmtplib to assert dispatch success path.
critical_code_review_comments: services/alerts.py: dedup uses asyncio.get_event_loop().time() which is monotonic — safe; but the map is process-local. Acknowledged as fine for single-worker uvicorn; document this limitation in the module docstring or move dedup to a Mongo TTL collection.

services/alerts.py _email_send: start_tls=True for port 25 will fail against many relays that don't advertise STARTTLS; consider making start_tls/use_tls explicit via env (e.g. SMTP_TLS=starttls|ssl|none) rather than inferring from port.

routers/alerts.py PUT /alerts/prefs uses Pydantic AlertPrefs but defaults event_types to None and lets the service fall back to DEFAULT_HIGH_EVENT_TYPES — works, but if a user explicitly sends event_types=[] the server replaces it with the full default list (because of 'or DEFAULT...' truthy check). If the product intent is 'subscribe to nothing', use `if event_types is None` instead of `or`.

services/audit.record_event auto-dispatch hook fires for every HIGH event — confirm it's awaited in a fire-and-forget asyncio.create_task() (not blocking the API request) — otherwise the PUT /risk/limits latency will include the alert dispatch round-trip. Current implementation appears non-blocking based on observed sub-second PUT response.
AlertsPanel.jsx sendTest() always pre-saves prefs before testing — if the save fails, the test still proceeds and the user gets a confusing error. Wrap in try/await save then early-return on failure.
test_credentials: demo@algoforge.io / Demo@123 (admin). Per-run alerts_<uuid>@test.io trader registered for default-prefs test.
seed_data_creation: Per-run: 1 fresh user via /api/auth/register for GET-defaults coverage. Alert_log rows from kill-switch toggle + dedup tests left in DB (acceptable — they are observability data, not seed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 11 alerts pipeline is GREEN. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_alerts_iter11.py covers the 4 alerts endpoints, audit auto-dispatch hook, 60s dedup, and admin/health alerts block. Transports (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, SMTP_*) intentionally unset — feature works in degraded mode (test endpoint returns 400 with descriptive error, audit dispatch writes ok=false rows to alert_log). When transports get wired up later, add success-path tests by monkeypatching httpx.AsyncClient + aiosmtplib.send. Login response key is access_token (not token) across all auth fixtures. Frontend AlertsPanel testids: alerts-panel, alerts-telegram-enabled, alerts-telegram-chat-id, alerts-telegram-test, alerts-email-enabled, alerts-email-address, alerts-email-test, alerts-evt-<EVENT_TYPE>, alerts-save, alerts-refresh, alerts-log-row-<i>. Settings tabs: settings-tab-risk, settings-tab-alerts. Note: backend tests for dedup/kill-switch toggle leave telegram_enabled and email_enabled=True on the admin user — subsequent UI runs will see those persisted; not a defect.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. All P1 alerts acceptance criteria pass on first run.
